minecraft.title: Ratio Riddles
minecraft.desc: ratio, proportion, fractions & scale through 3 games
minecraft.subject: MATHEMATICS

minecraft.title: Dangerous Data Sets
minecraft.desc: probability & data using mob drop rates
minecraft.subject: MATHEMATICS

minecraft.title: Area and Volume
minecraft.desc: create & solve area/volume problems
minecraft.subject: MATHEMATICS

minecraft.title: Fractions Pixel Art
minecraft.desc: Design a pixel art image then break down the colors into fractions, discuss number patterns & unit fractions, then build the designs in Minecraft.
minecraft.subject: MATHEMATICS

minecraft.title: Number Pattern Architecture
minecraft.desc: explore arithmetic patterns through building
minecraft.subject: MATHEMATICS

minecraft.title: The Scientific Method
minecraft.desc: teach the scientific method through Minecraft experiments
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Shipwreck Narrative
minecraft.desc: write a narrative about a mysterious shipwreck
minecraft.subject: LANGUAGE & LITERACY

minecraft.title: Meet and Greet!
minecraft.desc: research historical figures, build 3D timeline
minecraft.subject: SOCIAL STUDIES & HISTORY

minecraft.title: Peter Is Here Ai For Cultural Heritage
minecraft.desc: How can we use technology to explore, protect, and celebrate cultural heritage sites, and why is it important to work together in this effort?
minecraft.subject: SOCIAL STUDIES & HISTORY

minecraft.title: Peace Builders
minecraft.desc: Peace Builders offers new perspectives from four more laureates and focuses on avoiding and mitigating conflict. Learners will gain perspective on how to work together toward a more peaceful world.
minecraft.subject: SOCIAL STUDIES & HISTORY

minecraft.title: Ocean Heroes
minecraft.desc: Join the Ocean Heroes team in a thrilling underwater adventure to rescue coral reefs, mangroves, and kelp forests. Learn real-world conservation skills while protecting marine biodiversity!
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: The Investigators
minecraft.desc: Can you trust the sources of your information? Go on a quest for the facts! Become a school reporter and investigate 3 unique stories. Evaluate biases, decode clues, and uncover the truth!
minecraft.subject: SOCIAL STUDIES & HISTORY

minecraft.title: Active Citizen
minecraft.desc: Minecraft: Education Edition?s Active Citizen world which explores the incredible stories of Nobel Peace Laureates and how they achieved amazing things.
minecraft.subject: SOCIAL STUDIES & HISTORY

minecraft.title: The Hall of Inventions
minecraft.desc: Explore this museum that features key innovations from history. However, there are blank spaces across time. This is where you come in. Consider what you think are the most important inventions, and then use the build plots to build them yourself.
minecraft.subject: ARTS & DESIGN

minecraft.title: Historical Homes
minecraft.desc: Travel through time in this build challenge and explore architecture from ancient, medieval, and modern times.
minecraft.subject: SOCIAL STUDIES & HISTORY

minecraft.title: Artemis Rocket Build
minecraft.desc: Blast off to the moon with this superstellar map created in partnership with NASA. Meet scientists and engineers, explore jet propulsion, and design a rocket. Test your creation with a fiery launch!
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Planet Earth Iii Lesson Collection2
minecraft.desc: Play as a series of animals like the Cape fur seal and great white shark, the musk ox and Arctic wolf, and many more. Explore locations from the Arctic tundra to the Okavango Delta.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Filtering Water On The Iss
minecraft.desc: Students will experiment with creating a water filtering system as well as learn how water is filtered on the International Space Station.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Bone Loss In Space
minecraft.desc: Students will complete an experiment to understand the effects of bone loss in space and graph the data in Minecraft.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Plants Vs Gravity
minecraft.desc: Students learn about how gravity affects plants on Earth as well as in space. They then participate in a hands-on planting experiment.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Invisible Forces At Work
minecraft.desc: Students will review magnetism and gravity. They will design a magnetic structure that could protect the International Space Station or assist its astronauts.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Rockets Away
minecraft.desc: Students will learn about rockets and how astronauts get to the International Space Station on one. They will also build a hands-on rocket as well as one in Minecraft.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Seeing Earth From The Iss
minecraft.desc: Students will recreate an area of Earth in Minecraft after exploring images of Earth from the International Space Station.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Solar Panel Setback
minecraft.desc: Students will explore how solar energy powers the International Space Station. They will also understand how robotic tools help astronauts fix items in space.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Moon Base One
minecraft.desc: Utilize your creativity and collaboration skills to build a sustainable lunar base to support efficient life systems and long-term lunar habitation!
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: James Webb Space Telescope Challenge
minecraft.desc: Peer into the world's most advanced telescope to explore the universe, craft constellations and create a solar system. Developed with NASA to inspire the next generation of cosmic adventurers.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Its Good To Be Green
minecraft.desc: Students explore a sustainable building.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Sustainable Food Production
minecraft.desc: Begin at a grocery store where farm products are bought and sold. Explore a sustainable farm and learn about soil conservation, crop rotation, and water pollution. Visit a waste facility and learn how fertile soil is created from waste products. Walk through a recycling facility and learn how old packaging is recycled into new packaging. Visit the shop where the farm products are being packaged and sold back to the grocery store.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Sustainable Home
minecraft.desc: Students will? Explore a home that has been built with sustainable materials. Discover what materials are sustainable and where those materials come from. Understand what can be done to a home to help it be efficient.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Radical Recycling
minecraft.desc: Gain background knowledge about recycling through introductory games and a challenge.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Dependable Forests
minecraft.desc: Students will? Explore a sustainable forest in order to understand how it can be used consistently. Understand the importance of protecting forests not only for the trees, but for wildlife as well. Understand that sustainable forestry practices have a social, economic, and environmental impact.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Alternative Energy
minecraft.desc: Explore hydropower, wind power, and waste-to-energy. Understand how mechanical and electrical energy play a part in alternative energy. Explore how the energy created at the power plant gives energy back to the community. Learn how landfills can be sustainable using garbage. Explore what happens to recyclable and non-recyclable materials once they leave the home. Understand how non-recyclables can be turned into energy.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Outflow Order
minecraft.desc: Explore how outflow (wastewater) gets from a home to a water treatment plant. Understand that biosolids are used in composting. Learn how outflow is treated in order to go back into the city system.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Frozen Planet Ii Frozen Worlds
minecraft.desc: Students are introduced to the killer whale, polar bear, and Lapland bumblebee. They will develop an understanding of how these animals have adapted to survive extreme conditions.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Frozen Planet Ii Frozen Peaks
minecraft.desc: Students are introduced to the high-casqued chameleon and the golden eagle. They will develop an understanding of how they have adapted to survive extreme conditions.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Frozen Planet Ii Frozen Lands2
minecraft.desc: Students are introduced to the Lapland bumblebee and the Amur leopard. They will develop an understanding of how they have adapted to survive extreme conditions.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Frozen Planet Ii Frozen South
minecraft.desc: Explore animals living in the Frozen South and examine how climate change is making their survival more difficult.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Frozen Planet Ii Frozen Ocean
minecraft.desc: Explore the Arctic Ocean and its wildlife. See how the habitats and behaviors of animals has changed with the effect of climate change.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: An Ecosystem Jigsaw
minecraft.desc: Students will understand how habitat loss might affect the relationship between the leopard and impala, as well as the grazing and hunting habits of the impala and leopard, respectively.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: The Arctic Wolf Musk Ox Challenge
minecraft.desc: Understand the characteristics of Arctic wolves and musk oxen. Describe the relationship and interactions between Arctic wolves and musk oxen. Consider how actual and hypothetical changes could affect the interconnectedness of these two animals.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Fur Seal Great White Shark Showdown
minecraft.desc: Understand how Cape fur seals defend themselves, their pups, and their territory against great white sharks Understand how great white sharks hunt for Cape fur seals and how solitary hunting can be difficult Consider how humans can protect areas in which both the Cape fur seals and great white sharks live (optional activity)
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Mutualistic Vibes
minecraft.desc: Understand the characteristics of treehoppers and stingless bees. Explain how treehoppers communicate with their young (nymphs). Be able to describe how stingless bees help certain species of treehoppers keep their offspring safe. Explore deforestation and how that affects the ecosystem of the treehopper and stingless bee (optional activities).
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Lily Trotter Nest Challenge
minecraft.desc: Understand the characteristics of lily-trotter fathers and their chicks. Become familiar with where the Okavango Delta is and how it is a unique ecosystem. Explore the challenges of lily-trotter fathers in caring for their young from eggs to hatched chicks.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Climate Futures The Forest
minecraft.desc: To develop an understanding of deforestation and the pitfalls of unsustainable industrialization (SDG 9)  To explore how deforestation is connected to the greenhouse effect (SDG 13)  To identify how human activities contribute to climate change (SDG 13)
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Climate Futures Transportation
minecraft.desc: To learn how the development of transportation has contributed to climate change resulting in increased greenhouse gas emissions (SDG 13)  Explore the impact of increased transportation and the conflict in achieving sustainability in infrastructure and industrialization (SDG 9)  Develop an awareness of sustainable innovation to prevent, mitigate or adapt the impact of transportation on climate change (SDG 9 and 13)
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Climate Futures The Farm
minecraft.desc: To understand agriculture, the food supply chain and conflicts in achieving sustainability in infrastructure and industrialization. Explore the impact mass food production has on climate change locally, nationally, and globally (SDG 13)  Identify strategies to prevent, mitigate or reduce the impact on the environment of mass agriculture (SDG 13)
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Watr Meet The Rangers
minecraft.desc: Obtain and combine information about ways individual communities use science ideas to protect the Earth?s resources and environment.  Explore day to day duties of a Wildlife Ranger in the conservation of animals and their habitats  Develop an understanding of the role of a Ranger in wildlife conservation
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Watr Humans And Elephants
minecraft.desc: Students develop an understanding of the complex issues surrounding habitat destruction and land-use conflict  Students use case studies to identify solutions that have been tried and tested ? and use tools to assess their relative success  NGSS - HS-LS2-7. Design, evaluate, and refine a solution for reducing the impacts of human activities on the environment and biodiversity.  Students use their developing understanding of the needs and preferences of both elephant and human communities to design and trial a range of solutions in Minecraft
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Running With Rhinos
minecraft.desc: Students develop an understanding of the different species and sub species of African rhinos.  Students explore the different requirements, behaviors and preferences of these species and use this knowledge to plan and build a suitable sanctuary.  NGSS - MS-ESS3-3. Apply scientific principles to design a method for monitoring and minimizing a human impact on the environment.*
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Detecting Wildlife Crime
minecraft.desc: Students explore the operation of animal trafficking at a working port. They investigate a crime by interacting with staff on-site to stop trafficking
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Conservation Science
minecraft.desc: Students take part in a DNA matching operation, sourcing dung and ivory to assist in identifying where elephants are most at risk.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: BUILD WITH BEES: BUMBLE BUZZ
minecraft.desc: Students learn about the huge diversity of bumble bee species using an online community science database and observations in their own schoolyard.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Bee A Hero
minecraft.desc: After hearing farmers? and beekeepers? stories on the Farm, students embark on their own bee advocacy projects.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Farming
minecraft.desc: Students explore dynamic, multi-faceted solutions to the difficult and complicated issue of bee death, using sustainable agriculture as a guide.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Pollination
minecraft.desc: Students dissect a flower to explore how its structures and functions relate to a pollinator?s physical and behavioral adaptations.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Bee Nests
minecraft.desc: Focusing on different bees species? nesting sites and practices, students explore how unique adaptations help bees survive in a variety of settings.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Habitat
minecraft.desc: Students apply their knowledge of bees? lifelong needs to build Minecraft ?beetopias? to support and grow their very own bee colonies.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Life Cycle
minecraft.desc: Students explore the life cycle of bees, learning what the bees need to thrive -- and what can threaten survival -- at each stage.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Beeswax
minecraft.desc: Students learn how bees make and use wax, then they explore tessellation and perimeter-to-area ratios to find out why bees build hexagons.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Honey
minecraft.desc: How honey is made, & why. Students practice observational skills and become honey connoisseurs by exploring honey?s wide range of colors and tastes.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Bee Treasures
minecraft.desc: Students learn about what bees produce in the hive: honey, beeswax, propolis, bee bread, and royal jelly, and how humans use these products, too.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Build With Bees Anatomy
minecraft.desc: Students compare characteristics common to all insects with a honeybee?s anatomy before using this information to design and build their own bees.
minecraft.subject: SCIENCE

minecraft.title: Creature Crafter
minecraft.desc: In this creative build challenge you will discover information about the life and legacy of Mary Shelley, the brilliant mind behind Frankenstein. Explore a thrilling laboratory and bring your imagination to life by crafting your own fantastical creature?build from scratch or remix Minecraft mobs. Unleash your inner creator in this fun and educational adventure!
minecraft.subject: LANGUAGE & LITERACY

microbit.title: Barefoot Litter Hunt
microbit.desc: Two lessons aimed at students aged 9-11. Students use buttons and sensors on the micro:bit to make different images display, are introduced to the concept of variables, and create a device that records details of litter that can be recycled in the local area.
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Barefoot Wildlife Animations
microbit.desc: Two lessons aimed at students aged 7-9. Students create icons and animations by programming the micro:bit's LED display and using the forever block and count-controlled loops.
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Being Active
microbit.desc: Three simple projects to learn about the importance of being active to help prevent heart disease and create wearable devices to encourage people to be more active. Design challenges for finding solutions to the Global Goals for sustainable development (SDGs).
microbit.subject: SEL & LIFE SKILLS

microbit.title: Computing Fundamentals
microbit.desc: Six lessons designed for students aged 11-12 years. Students are introduced to the basics of computational thinking, programming and computer systems to ensure a shared level of understanding.
microbit.subject: CODING & COMPUTER SCIENCE

microbit.title: CreateAI Taster Lessons
microbit.desc: Two lessons introducing the concepts of AI and machine learning using micro:bit CreateAI.
microbit.subject: CODING & COMPUTER SCIENCE

microbit.title: Cryptography
microbit.desc: Three lessons designed for students aged 11-14 years to follow on from the ?Cyber security unit,? unit and a good first-introduction to text-based programming. Students are introduced to the fascinating world of cryptography through unplugged and programming activities.
microbit.subject: CODING & COMPUTER SCIENCE

microbit.title: Cyber Security
microbit.desc: Three lessons designed for students aged 11-14 years introducing cyber security. Students explore the need to create strong passwords before writing algorithms and programs to create their own ?strong password generator? using the BBC micro:bit.
microbit.subject: CODING & COMPUTER SCIENCE

microbit.title: Data Handling
microbit.desc: Five lessons aimed at students aged 9-10 years. Students explore data and the sensors on the BBC micro:bit through a variety of unplugged and programming activities.
microbit.subject: MATHEMATICS

microbit.title: Digital Flashcards
microbit.desc: Five lessons aimed at students aged 7-8 years. Students design and program digital flashcards to help them learn a foreign language using the BBC micro:bit.
microbit.subject: CODING & COMPUTER SCIENCE

microbit.title: Electrical Conductors
microbit.desc: Five lessons aimed at students aged 8-9 years. Students develop their understanding of flowchart algorithms, selection, inputs and outputs by programming the BBC micro:bit to make a tester for electrical conductivity.
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Energy Awareness
microbit.desc: Gather data about energy use around you using the BBC micro:bit as a simple data logger, then process and analyse the data to make informed decisions about reducing energy usage
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Getting Active
microbit.desc: Five lessons aimed at students aged 10-11. Students are introduced to variables and develop different programs using the BBC micro:bit to help families get active.
microbit.subject: SEL & LIFE SKILLS

microbit.title: Health Tech
microbit.desc: Five lessons designed for students aged 11-14 years to follow on from the ?Computing fundamentals? unit. Students work in teams to design and prototype their own health tech innovation.
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Healthy Oceans
microbit.desc: Two longer, advanced projects to explore how wireless networks, smart materials and algorithms can help keep our oceans clean. Design challenges for finding solutions to the Global Goals for sustainable development (SDGs).
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Helping Plants Grow
microbit.desc: Two advanced projects to explore how technology can protect plant life and increase food production. Design challenges for finding solutions to the Global Goals for sustainable development (SDGs).
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Musical Micro:bit
microbit.desc: Five lessons designed for students aged 9-10. Students compose musical phrases and algorithms before exploring how the BBC micro:bit can be programmed to play music.
microbit.subject: ARTS & DESIGN

microbit.title: Nature Art
microbit.desc: This unit of four lessons is suitable for students aged 7-8 years. They create nature representations, firstly using art materials and are introduced to computational thinking and programming the LEDs on the BBC micro:bit.
microbit.subject: ARTS & DESIGN

microbit.title: Night Safety
microbit.desc: Three simple projects to learn about the importance of road safety at night and create wearable devices to help young people ?Be Safe: Be Seen!? Design challenges for finding solutions to the Global Goals for sustainable development (SDGs).
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Protecting Animals
microbit.desc: Two simple activities exploring how technology can be used to protect biodiversity in your neighbourhood and the wider world using the BBC micro:bit. Design challenges for finding solutions to the Global Goals for sustainable development (SDGs).
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Sensory Classroom
microbit.desc: Four lessons designed for students aged 11-14 years. Students explore sensory classrooms and design and create their own sensory learning aids using the BBC micro:bit.
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Volcano Animations
microbit.desc: Five lessons aimed at students aged 8-9 years. Students explore animations, developing their computational thinking and use repetition to create volcanic eruption animations using the LEDs on the BBC micro:bit.
microbit.subject: SCIENCE

microbit.title: Saving Sea Creatures
microbit.desc: Two simple projects to learn about the threats faced by fish and sea turtles and how technology can help them survive and promote bio-diversity. Design challenges for finding solutions to the Global Goals for sustainable development (SDGs).
microbit.subject: SCIENCE
